$comment: Zero Two One agent tool definitions. Schemas follow the Anthropic tool-use input_schema format so they can be registered directly with an agent runtime. The non-standard `x_command` field maps each tool to its local CLI implementation for harnesses that execute tools via shell (substitute {placeholders} with the input values).

tools.name: fetch_speckit_context
tools.description: Retrieve the current feature's Spec Kit requirements and inject them into the agent's context. Reads specs/NNN-feature-name/ (spec, plan, research, data model, contracts, tasks, checklists) and generates .ai/context/NNN-feature-name.md (a single AI-readable markdown bundle) plus .ai/context/NNN-feature-name.json (structured: status, gate state, acceptance criteria, entities, task progress). Call this BEFORE starting any implementation work on a feature, and re-call after spec artifacts change. When 'spec' is omitted, the feature is derived from the current NNN-feature-name git branch.

spec.description: Spec identifier: a number ('1', '001'), a full directory name ('001-user-onboarding'), or a name prefix. Omit to derive from the current feature branch.

tools.name: verify_spec_compliance
tools.description: Validate that a feature's Spec Kit artifact set is complete, unambiguous, and cleared for implementation, and that generated work still adheres to the downloaded spec definitions. Checks: spec resolves for the branch; spec.md declares a lifecycle status; status passes the implementation gate (Approved | Ready for Dev | In Progress | Done); plan.md/tasks.md exist; no unresolved [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] markers; a 'Done' spec has no unchecked tasks; the .ai/context bundle is fresh. Returns findings with PASS/WARN/FAIL levels; exit code 1 on any FAIL. Run after generating components or logic, and before marking a spec Done.

spec.description: Spec identifier (number, directory name, or prefix). Omit to derive from the current feature branch.

gate_only.description: Run only the fast gate checks (spec exists + status passes). Default false — full compliance audit.

json.description: Emit machine-readable JSON findings instead of the human report. Default true for agent use.

tools.name: sync_design_tokens
tools.description: Ingest a Figma design-token export (W3C Design Tokens JSON or a flat name→value map) and synchronize it into the codebase's token architecture: requirements/_design/tokens/tokens.json (source of truth) and tokens.css (generated :root custom properties). Existing token names are preserved as the design system's public API — values update in place, new tokens are added, and tokens absent from the import are reported as orphaned but kept unless prune is set. Use dry_run first to review the diff, then apply. Run whenever a designer reports Figma updates, or during Phase 2 (Pre-build) prototype styling.

input_file.description: Path to the Figma token export JSON file.

dry_run.description: Report the add/change/orphan diff without writing files. Default false.

prune.description: Remove tokens that are absent from the import. Destructive — confirm with the user first. Default false.

tools.name: set_spec_status
tools.description: Advance a feature spec through its lifecycle (Draft → In Review → Approved → Ready for Dev → In Progress → Done) by updating the status declared in specs/NNN-feature-name/spec.md. This status is the single source of truth for the pre-commit refinement gate. Only set 'Approved' or 'Ready for Dev' when the user (or a recorded review round) has explicitly signed off on the spec.

spec.description: Spec identifier (number, directory name, or prefix).
